must_have.name: Core Directive Injection
must_have.justification: Foundational security control ensuring core directives are injected at session start. Non-negotiable for policy enforcement across all stages. DORA Art. 9(4) requires documented ICT security policies.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Blocks all downstream controls if missing
must_have.risk_without: Complete failure of policy enforcement framework; uncontrolled agent behavior

must_have.name: Audit Trail & Compliance Reporting
must_have.justification: Required for audit trail and incident reporting compliance. Mandatory for DORA Art. 17 (ICT incident management) and EU AI Act Art. 12 (record-keeping). Non-negotiable for regulatory proof.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing audit trail violates regulatory requirements
must_have.risk_without: Non-compliance with incident reporting timelines (4h/72h/1mo); inability to prove AI system monitoring

must_have.name: End-to-End Traceability
must_have.justification: Foundational traceability across entire SDLC. Required by DORA Art. 8(1) (document all ICT-supported functions and assets) and EU AI Act Art. 12 (logging of AI system usage). Critical for demonstrating compliance.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Impossible to prove end-to-end compliance without traceability
must_have.risk_without: Cannot demonstrate data governance; audit failures

must_have.name: Agent Provenance Registry
must_have.justification: Required for third-party oversight (DORA Art. 28) and deployer obligations (EU AI Act Art. 26(5)). Essential for supply chain security and monitoring AI systems.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing third-party/agent oversight creates compliance gap
must_have.risk_without: Unable to manage ICT third-party risk; breach of deployer monitoring obligations

must_have.name: Specification Validation
must_have.justification: Establishes source of truth for all agentic actions. Prevents propagation of invalid requirements downstream. Foundational for quality and compliance.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Invalid specs cascade into all subsequent stages
must_have.risk_without: Misaligned development; rework across multiple stages

must_have.name: Risk Classification
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 8(2) to continuously identify ICT risk sources and assess cyber threats. Foundational for AI risk management (EU AI Act Art. 9). Cannot proceed without risk tier.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Risk management cannot proceed without classification
must_have.risk_without: Unmanaged risks; inability to apply appropriate governance to high-risk AI systems

must_have.name: AI Risk Tier Classification
must_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 6 (classification rules for high-risk AI) and Art. 9 (risk management system). Determines entire compliance regime. Non-negotiable for AI systems.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Determines which AI Act requirements apply
must_have.risk_without: Unknown whether system is high-risk; potential serious violations if misclassified as low-risk

must_have.name: Intent Traceability
must_have.justification: Establishes starting point for end-to-end traceability required by DORA Art. 8(1) and EU AI Act Art. 12. Source of truth for audit trail.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Root traceability point; missing here breaks downstream audit trail
must_have.risk_without: Incomplete audit trail; cannot trace back to original intent

must_have.name: Pre-Guardrails
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 9 (ICT security policies) and EU AI Act Art. 15(4) (resilience against manipulation). Prevents malicious intent from entering development cycle.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Prevents prohibited/harmful systems from starting development
must_have.risk_without: Uncontrolled system scope; potential development of prohibited AI systems

must_have.name: Design Completeness & Standards
must_have.justification: Ensures design meets standards before coding begins. Critical gate to prevent costly rework. Required for quality assurance.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Incomplete designs cascade to implementation rework
must_have.risk_without: Technical debt; missing architectural requirements

must_have.name: Design Approval
must_have.justification: Human-required control ensuring design risk is formally assessed before coding. Critical governance gate blocking code implementation.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Blocks deployment of risky designs
must_have.risk_without: High-risk designs proceed to implementation without approval

must_have.name: Threat Model Validation
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 25 (testing methodologies) and EU AI Act Art. 9 (risk management). Threat model is foundation for security testing and mitigation.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Security testing roadmap depends on threat model
must_have.risk_without: Security testing lacks direction; unidentified threat vectors

must_have.name: Stage Directive Injection
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 9(4) and EU AI Act Art. 14 (human oversight). Injects stage-specific directives governing agent behavior. Non-negotiable for human oversight and policy enforcement.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing stage directives break oversight chain
must_have.risk_without: Uncontrolled agent behavior; missing human oversight mandates

must_have.name: AI Component Design Review
must_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 10 (data governance), Art. 13 (transparency), and Art. 14 (human oversight). Critical gate for AI systems to address data quality, transparency, and oversight.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing AI design review breaches multiple AI Act articles
must_have.risk_without: Unaddressed data governance; missing transparency and oversight mechanisms

must_have.name: Permission Management
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 9(4) (access controls and authentication). Prevents unauthorized code changes and enforces least privilege.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing access controls violate DORA Art. 9 requirements
must_have.risk_without: Uncontrolled code access; potential security breaches during implementation

must_have.name: Post-Guardrails
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 9 and EU AI Act Art. 15(4) (resilience against manipulation). Detects and prevents malicious code or policy violations in final code.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Post-guardrails prevent malicious code from reaching testing
must_have.risk_without: Malicious or non-compliant code passes through to testing and deployment

must_have.name: Secrets & Credentials Scanning
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 28 (supply chain security) and EU AI Act Art. 15(3) (supply chain integrity). Prevents exposure of sensitive credentials.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Exposed credentials cause immediate compliance breach
must_have.risk_without: Credential exposure; potential supply chain compromise

must_have.name: Code Provenance Tracking
must_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 17(1)(f) (data/documentation requirements) and DORA Art. 28 (third-party oversight). Essential for supply chain security and AI audit trail.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Cannot prove code provenance for regulatory audit
must_have.risk_without: Unknown code origins; cannot identify malicious or third-party contributions

must_have.name: SAST
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 24-25 (digital operational resilience testing). Static analysis is foundational security testing for identifying code-level vulnerabilities.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - SAST is baseline security testing requirement
must_have.risk_without: Code-level vulnerabilities not identified before testing/deployment

must_have.name: DAST
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 24-25 (operational resilience testing including penetration testing). Dynamic analysis is foundational for runtime security validation.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - DAST is baseline security testing requirement
must_have.risk_without: Runtime/deployment vulnerabilities not identified before production

must_have.name: Risk Threshold Evaluation
must_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 9(7) (testing to verify risk management measures). Blocks deployment if residual risk exceeds tolerance. Critical gate.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Blocks high-risk deployment; gates stage exit
must_have.risk_without: Unmanaged risks proceed to deployment; no go/no-go decision

must_have.name: Documentation Completeness
must_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 11 (technical documentation) and Annex IV. Non-negotiable for regulatory proof of AI system compliance.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing documentation violates EU AI Act Art. 11
must_have.risk_without: Cannot prove regulatory compliance; market access blocked

must_have.name: CAB Approval
must_have.justification: Required by change management governance (DORA Art. 8(5)). Human-required control ensuring deployment decision is formally authorized.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Blocks unauthorized deployments
must_have.risk_without: Uncontrolled deployments; bypass of change management procedure

must_have.name: Infrastructure Security
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 24-25 (testing of infrastructure). Ensures production environment meets security requirements before deployment.
must_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Infrastructure vulnerabilities expose production systems
must_have.risk_without: Production deployment to insecure infrastructure

must_have.name: Deployment Integrity
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 28 (supply chain security) and EU AI Act Art. 15(3) (supply chain integrity). Ensures code/model integrity during deployment.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Deployment tampering bypasses all prior controls
must_have.risk_without: Tampered code deployed to production; prior security controls negated

must_have.name: Incident Scanning & Detection
must_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 10 (detection mechanisms) and Art. 17-19 (incident management and reporting). Critical for incident identification and regulatory reporting.
must_have.remediation_impact: CRITICAL - Missing incident detection blocks regulatory reporting
must_have.risk_without: Incidents not detected; regulatory reporting timelines missed (4h/72h)

should_have.name: Coherence & Conflict Resolution
should_have.justification: High-value quality control preventing conflicting requirements upstream. Not blocking but significantly improves development efficiency and reduces rework.
should_have.remediation_impact: MEDIUM - Conflicting specs cause mid-cycle rework
should_have.risk_without: Inconsistent requirements lead to design conflicts downstream

should_have.name: Design Approval
should_have.justification: Human-required; see Must-Have category. Listed as Should in distribution due to redundancy with QC-2A gate.

should_have.name: Pull Request Creation & Review
should_have.justification: Important for code quality and peer review but can be supplemented with automated checks. Supports DORA Art. 8(5) (change management) but not blocking.
should_have.remediation_impact: MEDIUM - Code changes tracked but not strictly gated
should_have.risk_without: Code changes lack peer review; quality degradation possible

should_have.name: Code Quality Standards
should_have.justification: Automated enforcement of code quality. Improves maintainability and reduces technical debt. Not blocking deployment but significant for long-term maintainability.
should_have.remediation_impact: MEDIUM - Technical debt accumulates without code quality standards
should_have.risk_without: Code quality degradation; increased maintenance cost

should_have.name: Decision Log
should_have.justification: Supports audit trail and compliance reporting (GC-0A). Important for demonstrating decision rationale but not blocking deployment.
should_have.remediation_impact: MEDIUM - Audit trail incomplete without decision log
should_have.risk_without: Cannot demonstrate decision-making rationale; audit gaps

should_have.name: Functional & Non-Functional Testing
should_have.justification: Critical quality gate but automated testing tooling can substitute some manual testing. Required for verification but gates compliance more through exit criteria.
should_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Missing functional testing prevents verification gate
should_have.risk_without: Untested functionality reaches deployment; failures in production

should_have.name: AI Output Validation
should_have.justification: Important for AI systems but only applicable to AI components. Supports EU AI Act Art. 15 (accuracy/robustness) but not blocking if no AI present.
should_have.remediation_impact: HIGH (for AI) - Missing AI output validation breaches Art. 15
should_have.risk_without: Unvalidated AI outputs; accuracy/robustness not verified

should_have.name: Rollback Plan Validation
should_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 11 (ICT business continuity). Important for recovery but gates exit through rollback readiness.
should_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Missing rollback plan blocks recovery capability
should_have.risk_without: No recovery path if deployment fails; business continuity gap

should_have.name: Release Readiness Checklist
should_have.justification: Comprehensive gate checklist supporting change management (DORA Art. 8(5)). Important but gates through exit criteria.
should_have.remediation_impact: MEDIUM - Incomplete readiness checklist gates deployment

should_have.name: Risk & Health Monitoring
should_have.justification: Required by DORA Art. 10-11 (detection and monitoring) for continuous risk assessment. Important for ongoing compliance but monitoring rather than blocking.
should_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Missing risk monitoring breaks continuous compliance
should_have.risk_without: Unmonitored risk drift; regulatory violations go undetected

should_have.name: Bias & Fairness Testing
should_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 10 (data governance for bias), Art. 15 (robustness). Critical for high-risk AI but only applicable to AI components.
should_have.remediation_impact: HIGH (for AI) - Missing bias testing breaches Art. 10/15
should_have.risk_without: Undetected bias in AI systems; fairness violations; discrimination risk

should_have.name: AI Post-Market Surveillance
should_have.justification: Required by EU AI Act Art. 72 (post-market monitoring) and Art. 26(5) (deployer monitoring obligations). Critical for deployed AI systems but monitoring rather than blocking.
should_have.remediation_impact: HIGH - Missing AI surveillance violates Art. 72
should_have.risk_without: No monitoring of deployed AI behavior; serious incidents not detected

could_have.name: SLA/SLO Monitoring
could_have.justification: Efficiency optimization for operational monitoring. Nice-to-have for SLA tracking but not blocking. Supports Phase 3 optimization.
could_have.remediation_impact: LOW - Absence affects operational efficiency, not compliance
could_have.risk_without: SLA/SLO tracking requires manual effort; visibility reduced

could_have.name: Runtime Anomaly Detection
could_have.justification: Advanced monitoring for operational security. Complements SC-6A with enhanced detection but not blocking. Phase 3 optimization.
could_have.remediation_impact: LOW - Enhancement to SC-6A; not blocking if SC-6A present
could_have.risk_without: Anomaly detection less sophisticated; may miss subtle attacks

could_have.name: DAST
could_have.justification: Listed as Could; see Must-Have category. Dynamic testing is critical not optional.

could_have.name: Rollback Plan Validation
could_have.justification: Listed as Could; see Should-Have category. Rollback plan is required not optional.

critical_chains.chain: Intent → Risk Classification → Design → Threat Model → Security Testing → Deployment
critical_chains.impact: Missing any control in chain breaks verification gate

critical_chains.chain: AI Risk Tier → AI Design Review → Bias Testing → AI Surveillance
critical_chains.impact: Missing AI controls creates compliance gap for AI systems

critical_chains.chain: Directives (SC-0D, SC-2B) → Permission Management (SC-3A) → Post-Guardrails (SC-3B) → Incident Detection (SC-6A)
critical_chains.impact: Missing directive injection breaks entire security enforcement chain

critical_chains.chain: Audit Trail (GC-0A) → Traceability (GC-0B) → Code Provenance (GC-3A) → Agent Registry (GC-0C)
critical_chains.impact: Missing any governance control creates audit trail gaps

blocking_controls.rationale: Design approval gate prevents all stage 2 designs from proceeding
Risk threshold must pass before deployment phase
CAB approval required before any deployment actions

risk_without_must_haves.regulatory_compliance: CRITICAL - 8 article violations across DORA and EU AI Act
risk_without_must_haves.deployment_blockage: CRITICAL - Cannot legally deploy AI systems or handle incidents
risk_without_must_haves.audit_failure: CRITICAL - Cannot prove compliance to regulators
risk_without_must_haves.security_posture: CRITICAL - No incident detection, no security testing, no access controls
risk_without_must_haves.governance_gap: CRITICAL - No audit trail, no traceability, no third-party oversight
risk_without_must_haves.ai_systems_impact: CRITICAL - Cannot classify high-risk AI; cannot demonstrate governance

phase_1_completion.deployment_readiness: Can legally deploy with risk management

phase_2_enhancements.efficiency_gains: Automated monitoring, decision logs, enhanced testing
phase_2_enhancements.oversight_strengthening: Continuous monitoring, risk drift detection, AI surveillance

phase_3_optimization.operational_excellence: SLA tracking, advanced anomaly detection, predictive monitoring
